raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites evaluation of the draft's central thesis, contribution framing, and whether the human-evaluation result supports the stated conceptual takeaway.","R1.2":"Assessing what to think of the artifact centrally requires checking whether the argument moves coherently from study design and results to the discussion conclusions.","R1.3":"The draft's main conclusions depend on whether the reported forced-choice results actually support claims about physical fidelity and solver substitution.","R1.4":"The artifact makes broad claims about closing the gap with classical CFD and building on prior PINN literature, making contribution delta a central expected responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projection of this vague delegation must preserve uncertainty about the user's intended level of feedback and about facts not verifiable from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong generalizations from a narrow benchmark and human discrimination study, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The task is only to project responsibilities, not to critique, rewrite, or verify the artifact itself.","RX.4":"The draft recommends downstream engineering adoption, so responsibility includes attention to possible harms from unsupported technical conclusions.","RX.5":"The projection must remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites evaluation of the draft's central thesis, contribution framing, and whether the human-evaluation result supports the stated conceptual takeaway.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what to think of the artifact centrally requires checking whether the argument moves coherently from study design and results to the discussion conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The draft's main conclusions depend on whether the reported forced-choice results actually support claims about physical fidelity and solver substitution.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes broad claims about closing the gap with classical CFD and building on prior PINN literature, making contribution delta a central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this vague delegation must preserve uncertainty about the user's intended level of feedback and about facts not verifiable from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong generalizations from a narrow benchmark and human discrimination study, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to project responsibilities, not to critique, rewrite, or verify the artifact itself.
rationale.RX.4: The draft recommends downstream engineering adoption, so responsibility includes attention to possible harms from unsupported technical conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the provided artifact.
